title: Inclusivity for Women, Persons with Disabilities, and Vulnerable Groups Debate
overall_topic: Inclusivity for Women, Persons with Disabilities, and Vulnerable Groups

questions.topic: Strategies for Women's and Vulnerable Groups' Inclusion
questions.question: What specific strategies and policies will your party implement to increase the participation and representation of women, support the elderly without disrupting their social lives, and reduce economic and social pressures on women and persons with disabilities?

answers.summary: Will provide capacity-building training to empower women and build their self-confidence.

key_points.point: Provide training to build women's self-confidence and capacity.

answers.summary: Will support the elderly by preserving their social roles, providing housing for the destitute, and creating economic opportunities for those able to work.

key_points.point: Enable the elderly to share their knowledge with the next generation.
Provide housing options for elderly people without support.
Create economic opportunities for elderly individuals who are capable of working.
Organize elderly associations to protect their rights and well-being.

answers.summary: Focuses on merit-based political representation, having fielded 85 female and 7 PWD candidates in the current election.

key_points.point: Fielded 85 female candidates and 7 candidates with disabilities (including 2 women) in the 7th election.
Focuses on bringing forward capable candidates based on merit.

answers.summary: Will implement education and health policies tailored to support women and PWDs, and encourage private sector social responsibility.

key_points.point: Provide training and tailor economic sectors to support women and PWDs.
Implement health and education policies designed to accommodate PWDs.
Encourage and incentivize the private sector to produce educational materials for PWDs.
Change societal attitudes to reduce social pressures on PWDs.

questions.topic: Addressing Discrimination, GBV, and Social Protection
questions.question: How will your party address the gender pay gap, gender-based violence, and harmful traditional practices? What are your concrete strategies for social protection of the elderly, and how do you ensure meaningful political participation for women?

answers.summary: Will provide mindset training, maternal health packages, ensure equal pay, and claims high female representation in its executive and central committees.

key_points.point: Provide capacity-building training to change women's mindsets and build confidence.
Implement maternal health and hygiene packages.
Ensure equal pay and equal participation in the workforce.
Claims 16 out of 32 central committee members and 8 out of 15 executive committee members are women.

answers.summary: Highlights its record of appointing women to high leadership roles, enacting laws against harmful practices, and holding perpetrators accountable.

key_points.point: Achieved 51% female representation in the cabinet (12 out of 23 members).
Appointed a female mayor for Addis Ababa.
Enacted legal frameworks to prevent harmful traditional practices and hold perpetrators accountable.

answers.summary: Proposes using accumulated pension funds to create suitable work for those who become disabled early, providing medical equipment regionally, and adjusting pensions to inflation.

key_points.point: Use accumulated funds to create suitable work opportunities for citizens who become disabled before retirement.
Provide medical equipment (e.g., radiation, lab) at the regional level and free healthcare for the poor.
Adjust pension payments to match the purchasing power of the Birr.

answers.summary: Plans to revise the education policy to be practical and tech-focused to prevent dropouts, and will establish a financial system to support the elderly.

key_points.point: Revise the education policy to be practical and technology-focused.
Establish a financial system and better social security policies for the elderly.
Build schools in pastoralist and agrarian areas and ensure private sector social responsibility.

questions.question: To what extent have you included persons with disabilities in your party structures from the grassroots to the executive level? What is your policy regarding orphans and vulnerable children? When will the comprehensive disability law be implemented?

answers.summary: Believes a comprehensive disability law is needed, has fielded many PWD candidates, and has PWDs in its executive committee.

key_points.point: Advocate for a comprehensive law tailored to the specific needs of PWDs.
Fielded 844 candidates, with one-third being women and several PWDs.
Has two PWDs in its executive committee and PWDs leading disability wings.

answers.summary: Promises mandatory inclusive building designs, a 5% job quota for PWDs, and details its PWD candidate and leadership numbers.

key_points.point: Enact and enforce mandatory inclusive design laws for all government and private buildings.
Allocate a 5% job quota for PWDs.
Provide discounted health and medication services, and expand assistive technologies like Braille.
Fielded 315 candidates, including 135 women and 17 PWDs (5 female).
Has 2 PWDs in the executive committee and 5 in the central committee.

answers.summary: Has finalized the preparation of a comprehensive disability law, mandates a 2% candidate quota for PWDs, and claims 2% of its members are PWDs.

key_points.point: Finalized the preparation of a comprehensive disability policy/law and will implement it shortly after the election.
Revising the building proclamation and ensuring new corridor developments are accessible to PWDs.
Mandated a 2% quota for PWD candidates in the 7th national election.
Claims 2% of its 14 million members are PWDs.

answers.summary: Aims to change the cultural stigma around disability and has PWDs in regional leadership, though none in the federal executive committee.

key_points.point: Work to change the cultural perception that views disability as a curse or shame.
Has PWDs in leadership positions at regional and zonal wing levels, though none in the federal executive committee.

questions.question: What specific policies ensure the participation of PWDs, women, and the elderly across all sectors? What are your strategies to prevent gender-based violence? For Prosperity Party, when will the women's policy be ratified?

answers.summary: Uses a 50+1 women representation model, provides regular training, uses Braille for party documents, and plans digital reporting for GBV.

key_points.point: Operates on a 50+1 women representation model for decision-making.
Provides party programs and bylaws in Braille.
Will implement digital reporting systems to prevent and respond to gender-based violence.
Will give official recognition to sign language.

answers.summary: Mandates all ministries to include vulnerable groups in their policies, focuses on GBV prevention and rehabilitation, and will ratify the women's policy soon.

key_points.point: Mandated all federal ministries to include PWDs, the elderly, and vulnerable groups in their policies.
Drafted a comprehensive disability policy with stakeholder consultation.
Addresses GBV through prevention, legal action, medical support, and economic rehabilitation for victims.
Will ratify and implement the women's empowerment policy shortly.

answers.summary: Focuses on translating policies into practice, ensuring equal decision-making power, and tackling GBV starting from the family and school levels.

key_points.point: Ensure women, PWDs, and the elderly have equal opportunities in political representation and decision-making.
Tackle GBV by working at the family, school, and cultural levels.
Empower women to protect themselves and report violence immediately to medical centers.

answers.summary: Plans to revise laws, provide severe disability subsidies, make sign language nationwide, and establish independent justice institutions and rehab centers for GBV.

key_points.point: Provide special subsidies for citizens with severe disabilities.
Implement sign language nationwide across all media communications.
Revise laws, including domestic violence laws, to better address the nature of GBV.
Establish independent justice institutions and rehabilitation centers for GBV victims.

questions.question: The gender policy hasn't been ratified for 30 years. How certain are you that it will be ratified and implemented during this election period?

answers.summary: Acknowledges the delay due to extensive consultations but confirms the preparation is complete and it will be ratified shortly.

key_points.point: The policy preparation is complete after extensive consultations and will be ratified and implemented shortly.

questions.question: To Renaissance Party: You mentioned establishing associations free from the ruling party. Since civic associations are independent by nature, do political parties have the mandate to establish them?

answers.summary: Argues that current associations are used for political purposes by the ruling party and need to be truly independent to fight for justice.

key_points.point: Claims current associations are under the ruling party's influence and fail to fight for women's justice.
Believes associations will be more effective if they operate independently from government interference.

questions.question: What is being done regarding education and job opportunities for children and youth with intellectual disabilities, and how will you support their families who have to stay home to care for them?

answers.summary: Will provide tailored vocational training based on their capacities.

key_points.point: Provide tailored vocational training based on what individuals with intellectual disabilities can comprehend.

answers.summary: Has established inclusive resource centers and enacted laws for affirmative action in employment.

key_points.point: Included provisions for PWD education in the general education proclamation.
Established 1,621 inclusive resource centers for education.
Implementing affirmative action and special support measures for employment.

answers.summary: Will include them in policy-making and remove party dependency for accessing education and jobs.

key_points.point: Include individuals with intellectual disabilities in designing policies and school facilities.
Remove party dependency requirements for accessing education and employment.

answers.summary: Will support schools to accommodate them, provide tax incentives for supportive private institutions, and support their families.

key_points.point: Require schools to build systems and hire specially trained teachers to accommodate children with intellectual disabilities.
Provide tax exemptions and incentives for private health and education institutions that support them.
Provide special support to families caring for individuals with intellectual disabilities.

questions.question: To Prosperity Party: You mentioned a 60% tax reduction for organizations hiring PWDs. Are there any organizations that have actually hired 60% PWDs?

answers.summary: Clarified that the policy offers up to a 60% tax exemption as an incentive for companies that hire PWDs, not a requirement to hire a 60% PWD workforce.

key_points.point: The policy provides up to a 60% tax exemption for private institutions that hire PWDs, as an incentive.

questions.topic: Female PWD Candidates and Disability Models
questions.question: To Prosperity Party: How many female PWDs have you included as candidates? To all: Disability has historically been handled via a charity model; what model will your party follow?

answers.summary: Mandates a 2% quota for PWD candidates, including women.

key_points.point: Enforced a mandatory 2% quota for PWD candidates, which includes female PWDs.

answers.summary: Rejects the charity model in favor of a rights-based, developmental model focusing on job creation and education.

key_points.point: Follows a rights-based and developmental model rather than a charity model.
Focuses on creating job opportunities, education, and training with adequate budget allocation.

answers.summary: Will integrate PWDs into society with government support, but needs to conduct studies to determine the exact model.

key_points.point: Will conduct studies to determine the most appropriate model for supporting PWDs.

answers.summary: Rejects dependency on charity and advocates for using national resources to support PWDs.

key_points.point: Will utilize domestic national resources rather than relying solely on charity and dependency.

questions.question: Ethiopia signed the UNCRPD. How are you evaluating its implementation? Also, are the building and employment proclamations for PWDs actually being implemented?

answers.summary: Is incorporating UNCRPD principles into the new comprehensive law and monitoring the building proclamation, ensuring new corridor developments are accessible.

key_points.point: Incorporating UNCRPD principles into the upcoming comprehensive disability law.
Monitoring the building proclamation and ensuring new corridor developments in Addis Ababa and 60 cities are accessible.

answers.summary: Argues implementation is politicized and promises to include PWDs directly in evaluating and designing policies.

key_points.point: Claims the implementation of laws is politicized and only applied when it benefits the ruling system.
Will include PWDs directly in designing policies and procedures that work for them.

answers.summary: Acknowledges gaps in UNCRPD implementation due to budget constraints and promises to allocate adequate funding.

key_points.point: Acknowledges gaps in UNCRPD implementation primarily due to budget constraints.
Will allocate adequate budget to properly implement disability laws and support the elderly.

questions.question: To Prosperity Party: What are you doing about the killings, kidnappings, and rape of women while legal frameworks seem weak? Also, why are roads being built with open ditches that endanger PWDs?

answers.summary: Addresses GBV through awareness, legal accountability, and economic empowerment, and claims to be making services and infrastructure accessible.

key_points.point: Addresses GBV through community awareness, legal accountability using criminal and family laws, and economic empowerment.
Working to make educational and service locations accessible and providing tailored materials for PWDs.

questions.question: To Prosperity Party: When will you stand up against gender-based violence and harassment during elections, such as female candidates being splashed with mud by officials?

answers.summary: States that GBV is addressed through legal accountability and awareness, and notes that those convicted of GBV are denied amnesty.

key_points.point: Denies annual amnesty/pardons to individuals convicted of gender-based violence.
Focuses on legal accountability and community awareness rather than just emotional reactions.

questions.topic: Land for Charities and Elderly Support
questions.question: To Prosperity Party: Are charities that support PWDs, women, and children lacking land to operate? Also, what has PP done specifically for the elderly?

answers.summary: Claims the government has provided land to many charities like Mekedonia and is supporting the elderly with housing and private sector partnerships.

key_points.point: The government has provided land to numerous charitable organizations, such as Mekedonia, across various regions.
Supporting the elderly by providing housing options and partnering with the private sector to fill gaps.

questions.question: To ESDP: What exact percentage of women have you included in your structures? Also, given your ideology of wealth distribution, from which source will you distribute wealth to vulnerable groups?

answers.summary: States 33% of candidates are women, and wealth distribution will come from proper management of national resources, taxes, and loans.

key_points.point: Fielded 33% (85 in number) female candidates.
Will distribute wealth by properly managing national resources, taxes, and international loans/grants.
